results.question: Show the full subjet outline for ARP6110-P1 Project I.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARP6110-P1 Title: Project I Aliases: ARP6110, Project Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2028 Credit Hours: 5 Contact Hours: 35 hours Pre-Requisite: Completed 60 credit hours (excluding Art & Humanities subjects) Objective: To enable students to apply knowledge and skills gained from different courses through solving of real world problems; acquire technical skills through literature review, analysis, design, implementation, and testing/evaluation; and develop soft skills needed to carry out a real world project. Assessment: - Part 1 – General Effort, Project Design, Research Proposal: 25% - Part 1 – Presentation: 10% Course Contents: - Project planning, analysis and design - Research proposal - Presentation Course Learning Outcomes: - Develop the strategies for background study, analysis, design, implementation, and evaluation based on real-world problem. - Demonstrate communication skills in writing and presenting the project design and implementation. - Employ contemporary digital and numeracy tools to critically analyze problems in artificial intelligence related fields. - Demonstrate self-awareness in continuous lifelong-learning skills. - Apply integrated problem solving to propose a research paper/proposal for commercialization of the product. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Normalized from single ARP6110 code in structure.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARR6143 Robotics – Modelling and Control.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARR6143 Title: Robotics – Modelling and Control Aliases: Robotics - Modelling and Control Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2028 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provides students with rigorous mathematical skills on modelling and control of robotic arm and similar manipulator. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab Test: 20% - Final exam: 40% Laboratory: Control and automation lab, Robotic arms. Course Contents: - Representing position and orientation - Robot modeling - Forward and inverse kinematics - Trajectory generation - Velocity relationships - Dynamic and control Course Learning Outcomes: - Compute the pose of a robotic arm given the configuration, links and angle of each joint. - Deduce the joint angle of each robotic arm link given the required pose via inverse kinematic. - Generate the trajectory for the end-effector of a robotic arm via joint-space or Cartesian space schemes using anticipatory thinking. - Construct linear trajectory-following control scheme for robotic arm. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx
results.ui_answer: Subject Code: Title: Robotics – Modelling and Control Aliases: Robotics - Modelling and Control Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 1 Intake Cycle Label: March/April 2028 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: None shown in structure Objective: To provides students with rigorous mathematical skills on modelling and control of robotic arm and similar manipulator. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARR6153 Mobile Robots and Drones.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARR6153 Title: Mobile Robots and Drones Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 2 Intake Cycle Label: July/August 2028 Credit Hours: 3 Contact Hours: 44 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6123 Micro-controllers & Micro-processors; ARR6123 Robotics – Machine Design and Mechanisms Objective: To acquire knowledge to design, construct and program mobile robots and drones. Assessment: - Test: 20% - Assignment: 20% - Lab/Demonstration: 20% - Project: 40% Course Contents: - Introduction to mobile robots and drones - Wheeled robots - Balancing and legged robots - Drones Course Learning Outcomes: - Discuss the essential components and building blocks of mobile robots and drones. - Construct mobile robots or drones for solving a practical problem. - Develop mobile robots or drones using anticipatory thinking to perform a specific task. - Demonstrate entrepreneurial skills by writing a proposal for commercialization of the product/solution. - Demonstrate leadership, autonomy and responsibility in solving a practical problem. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.

results.question: What career can this robotics course lead to?
results.expected_answer: The MMU page lists these career prospects: Robotics System Designer/Programmers; AI and Machine Learning Developer; Embedded System Designer; Control and Automation Specialist; Field Application Technologist; Printed Circuit Board (PCB) Designer; Production and Planning Engineer; Industry 4.0 Technologist.
results.ui_answer: The MMU page lists these career prospects: Robotics System Designer/Programmers; AI and Machine Learning Developer; Embedded System Designer; Control and Automation Specialist; Field Application Technologist; Printed Circuit Board (PCB) Designer; Production and Planning Engineer; Industry 4.0 Technologist.

results.question: Show the full subject outline for ARC6184 Making Embedded Systems.?
results.expected_answer: Subject Code: ARC6184 Title: Making Embedded Systems Category: core Group: technical Year: 3 Trimester: 3 Intake Cycle Label: October/November 2028 Credit Hours: 4 Contact Hours: 60 hours (lectures, tutorials and laboratory experiments) Pre-Requisite: ARC6123 Micro-controllers & Micro-processors Objective: To introduce the best practice and the art of putting together an embedded system, from selection of processors, basic hardware requirements, to software design. Assessment: - Quiz: 20% - Test: 20% - Lab Test: 30% - Project: 30% Course Contents: - Introduction - Creating a system architecture - Getting on the hardware - Inputs, outputs and timers - Managing the flow of activity - Communicating with peripherals - Doing more with less - PCB design and layout - PCB fabrication, soldering and reworking Course Learning Outcomes: - Describe the hardware and software requirements of a robust embedded systems from datasheet. - Construct an embedded controller with support circuit from scratch. - Develop a software framework using systematic thinking for the embedded controller to perform a useful task. - Design PCB layout for an electronic prototype. Source Structure: BSc-IR-March-2026-T2610.pdf Source Detail: Subject Code all.docx Notes: - Prerequisite overridden by programme structure.
